questions.explanation: User interviews allow you to ask follow-up questions and uncover motivations, needs, and pain points in depth.
questions.question: Which user research method is best for understanding users' motivations and pain points through direct conversation?

questions.explanation: A user flow maps the sequence of steps/screens a user goes through to achieve a goal (e.g., sign up, purchase, submit a form).

options.A: A step-by-step path a user takes to complete a task
options.B: A set of brand colors and typography rules
options.C: A high-fidelity mockup with final visuals
options.D: A list of bugs found during QA

questions.question: In UX/UI work, what is a user flow?

questions.explanation: Wireframes focus on structure, content placement, and hierarchy before investing time in detailed visual design.

options.A: To define layout and information hierarchy without final visual styling
options.B: To finalize brand imagery and illustration style
options.C: To export production-ready assets for developers
options.D: To measure server performance under load

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a wireframe?

questions.explanation: Illustrator is built for vector graphics, making it the standard tool for scalable logos and icons.
questions.question: Which Adobe Creative Suite app is primarily used for vector logo design?

questions.explanation: Auto Layout helps UI elements adapt to content changes by maintaining consistent padding, spacing, and alignment.

options.A: It automatically resizes padding and spacing when the label text changes
options.B: It converts the button into a raster image for export
options.C: It guarantees WCAG compliance without further checks
options.D: It prevents the button from being reused in other files

questions.question: In Figma, what is the main benefit of using Auto Layout for a button component?

questions.explanation: Native date pickers constrain input to valid dates and match platform conventions, reducing formatting and entry errors.

options.B: A long paragraph explaining the date format
options.C: A hidden field that auto-fills without user awareness
options.D: A required free-text field with no validation

questions.question: Which interaction design pattern best reduces user errors when entering a date on mobile?

questions.explanation: Task-based usability testing observes real user behavior and identifies where users succeed, fail, or get confused in the flow.

options.A: Moderated usability testing with task-based scenarios
options.B: A brand preference survey about color palettes
options.C: Exporting the prototype to PDF and asking for comments
options.D: A stakeholder review focused only on visual polish

questions.question: You need to validate whether users can complete a checkout flow using a clickable prototype. Which approach is most appropriate?

questions.explanation: WCAG AA requires sufficient contrast (commonly 4.5:1 for normal text) to support users with low vision and in varied viewing conditions.

options.A: Ensuring body text color contrast meets at least 4.5:1 against the background
options.B: Using light gray text on white to look 'minimal'
options.C: Replacing headings with images of text to control typography
options.D: Disabling zoom to preserve the layout

questions.question: A marketing site must meet WCAG 2.2 AA. Which design choice most directly improves text readability for many users?

questions.explanation: Semantic tokens decouple intent from raw values, allowing the system to adjust scales per breakpoint while keeping component usage consistent.

options.A: Use semantic tokens (e.g., spacing-sm, text-body) mapped to base scale values that can change per breakpoint
options.B: Hardcode pixel values directly into every component and duplicate for each screen size
options.C: Use only one font size and one spacing value everywhere to avoid inconsistency
options.D: Let each designer choose their own spacing values per screen to speed up work

questions.question: You’re building a responsive design system for web and mobile. Which token strategy best supports consistent scaling across breakpoints?

questions.explanation: This workflow matches tools to tasks (vector, raster, motion) and supports consistency and speed through reusable templates and shared assets.

options.A: Design vector assets in Illustrator, edit photos in Photoshop, animate in After Effects, and assemble/export variations with consistent templates
options.B: Create everything in Premiere Pro because it can export images
options.C: Only use Photoshop for all tasks, including complex vector logos and animation
options.D: Skip templates and recreate each post from scratch to keep designs unique

questions.question: A client needs a social media campaign with static posts, short motion graphics, and a consistent brand look. Which workflow best uses Creative Suite efficiently?
